hypothesis: If residual risk is concentrated in deployable source buckets, a bounded admission rule should reduce proxy residual rates while retaining enough candidates to justify a local state-machine rerun.
interpretation: This is a source-attribution proxy, not a state-machine rerun; fee_after_pnl and stress are not recomputed after dropping a bucket.
The strongest proxy rule is late-offset source admission: offset_90_120 carries most residual while retaining most sample if removed.
Because hard cutoffs were previously killed in EC2 shadow, this KEEP only authorizes a local candidate-pipeline state-machine rerun, not another EC2 shadow or canary.
next_action: Run a local candidate-pipeline state-machine rerun from candidate_base with the same official-fee profile but a bounded late-offset admission variant equivalent to seed_offset_max_s=90, then compare sample, fee_after_pnl, stress100_worst_pnl, worst_day_fee_after_pnl, and residual rates. Do not run EC2 shadow or canary before this local rerun confirms the proxy.
